name: Nix code quality, readability, and docs

description: Refresh architecture module inventory to match current Darwin and Home modules

asi.hypothesis: Updating ARCHITECTURE.md's module inventory to match actual imports will improve human navigability and remove stale references such as modules/home/skills.nix.
asi.best_practice_basis: DeepWiki/Home Manager guidance emphasizes discoverable module organization and documented options/modules; this change makes the repo's module map truthful.
asi.adversarial_check: This is not benchmark-only: every added basename is path-qualified in the module tree with a short responsibility note, and a stale skills.nix reference was replaced by claude.nix.
asi.tooling_note: Using dedicated worktree /Users/martinfan/nix-config-autoresearch after earlier hidden/global worktree paths were pruned during failed autoresearch rollback.

description: Wrap long Nix source lines for readability

asi.hypothesis: Breaking long Nix source lines into named values, string concatenations, and wrapped shell commands will improve readability without behavior changes.
asi.rollback_reason: Correctness gate failed because tests/integration/configurations-eval-test.nix still needed nixpkgs-fmt after the edits; the idea itself is promising but must be reapplied with formatting before measurement.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply the long-line wrapping changes, run `nix fmt` immediately, then rerun the experiment. Include the same real readability edits: split brew assertion messages, font option text, Chrome Canary env paths, fomo alias, eza alias flags, fgb preview command, Gemini jq command, Sourcegraph Amp update hint, and long test messages.
asi.learned: The metric reached quality_debt=50 with long_nix_lines=0, so the hypothesis has high potential if formatting is fixed. Do not count this as kept until checks pass.

description: Reapply long-line readability wrapping with nix fmt

asi.hypothesis: Reapplying the long-line readability edits after nix fmt should pass the format gate while keeping long_nix_lines at zero.
asi.rollback_reason: format-test still reported tests/integration/configurations-eval-test.nix would be reformatted, so the local `nix fmt` command did not match the check's nixpkgs-fmt invocation for that file or did not apply the needed change.
asi.next_action_hint: Before reapplying, run `nixpkgs-fmt --check tests/integration/configurations-eval-test.nix` and `nixpkgs-fmt tests/integration/configurations-eval-test.nix` directly; inspect the specific diff. Then reapply only after the targeted format check is clean.
asi.learned: The readability edits still reduce quality_debt to 50, but repeated format failure means the next attempt must validate the exact check command, not just `nix fmt`.

description: Wrap long Nix source lines while preserving behavior

asi.hypothesis: Named constants, string concatenation, and wrapped shell commands can eliminate nearly all long Nix source lines while making intent clearer and preserving behavior.
asi.adversarial_check: Did not touch the one remaining long BetterMouse assertion line because the first attempt showed the formatter wanted a specific shape; keeping one clear line is better than fighting the formatter for a marginal point.
asi.quality_guard: Manual `nixpkgs-fmt --check` reported 0/69 would be reformatted before run_experiment; autoresearch checks passed.
asi.changes_summary: Split long brew assertion messages, font option text, Chrome Canary paths, climode Python fallback, fomo alias, tmux copy pipe, eza alias flags, fgb preview construction, Gemini jq command, Sourcegraph Amp update hint, format-test find command, and a long mkSystem assertion message.
asi.next_action_hint: Remaining quality_debt is the documented statix/deadnix contract gap. Next promising path: fix a small subset of statix warnings (empty patterns/manual inherit) or add a narrower static-lint gate only after making the checked subset clean.

description: Wire real statix/deadnix lint gate with scoped statix policy

asi.hypothesis: Fix actionable statix/deadnix findings, document a narrow statix repeated_keys opt-out, and add a real unit-static-lint check to close the documented lint-contract gap honestly.
asi.rollback_reason: Correctness gate timed out after 600s while fetching/building Nix dependencies for the expanded check set, before reporting an actual test failure. The benchmark metric and local direct statix/deadnix checks were promising, but the cycle did not complete.
asi.next_action_hint: Retry after cache warm-up, or first run the new `nix build .#checks.aarch64-darwin.unit-static-lint --no-link` directly. Keep the same edits: statix.toml disabling only repeated_keys with rationale; fix empty arg patterns in amp/crush/git/yazi; inherit cleanups in flake/tests/sourcegraph; remove unused zsh pkgs and opencode args; add unit-static-lint to tests/default.nix, README, and autoresearch.checks.sh.
asi.learned: Direct local checks before run_experiment passed for statix with `--ignore references/**` and deadnix over an explicit find-filtered Nix file list. Static-lint should avoid deadnix's fragile --exclude parsing by piping find output to xargs.
asi.timeout_detail: Checks spent the timeout fetching/building dependencies (NUR, git/perl stack, gh, etc.) after flake output changes; no failing assertion was shown.

description: Wire real statix/deadnix lint gate with scoped statix policy

asi.hypothesis: Fix actionable statix/deadnix findings, document a narrow statix repeated_keys opt-out, and add a real unit-static-lint check to close the documented lint-contract gap honestly.
asi.quality_guard: Prebuilt `.#checks.aarch64-darwin.unit-static-lint` before run_experiment to validate the new lint gate and warm the cache after the previous timeout; run_experiment checks then passed in 28.8s.
asi.statix_policy: Added statix.toml disabling only repeated_keys because this Home Manager repo intentionally keeps dotted `home.*` and `programs.*` declarations near activation comments and guards; all other statix rules remain active.
asi.changes_summary: Added unit-static-lint to tests/default.nix, tests/README.md, and autoresearch.checks.sh; fixed empty module args in amp/crush/git/yazi; cleaned inherit/manual arg warnings in flake.nix, tests/default.nix, sourcegraph-amp, zsh, opencode, and opencode-electron; deadnix scans only source files outside references via find/xargs.
asi.adversarial_check: This is not merely a metric mention: the check runs real statix and deadnix commands, excludes reference repos explicitly, and passes under the correctness gate.

description: Wrap final long assertion message for readability

asi.hypothesis: The last long test assertion message can be split into a normal Nix string concatenation, making the assertion easier to scan without changing behavior.
asi.quality_guard: Ran `nixpkgs-fmt --check tests/integration/configurations-eval-test.nix` before run_experiment; autoresearch checks passed in 27.5s.
asi.adversarial_check: This is a tiny readability edit, not benchmark cheating: it preserves the exact assertion message content and only wraps an over-140-character string in a test file.
asi.next_action_hint: Primary metric is now zero. Future iterations should improve the benchmark itself or pursue qualitative architecture/test improvements, not try to reduce the current metric further.

description: Audit saturated quality metric and refresh autoresearch notes

asi.hypothesis: With the current primary metric at its lower bound, the most honest next iteration is to verify the floor, refresh persisted session notes, and avoid fabricating a fake code improvement.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric was unchanged at 0, so there is no keepable improvement under the current objective. No source-code changes should be kept solely to create activity.
asi.next_action_hint: Reinitialize a broader quality metric before further optimization. Candidate signals: stale ARCHITECTURE Last reviewed date, stale documented module paths, option documentation coverage for custom modules, CI/check coverage parity, or autoresearch.md freshness. Do not make the current metric negative or remove checks to manufacture progress.
asi.learned: The current metric and checks are stable at quality_debt=0 with checks passing in 23.8s. Continued work needs a new measurement target or qualitative refactor with acceptance that the old metric cannot improve.
asi.preserved_state: Updated autoresearch.md What's Been Tried so a fresh agent sees the kept changes and the metric-floor guidance.

name: Nix documentation truthfulness and review freshness

asi.hypothesis: The previous quality_debt metric is saturated, so reinitialize around doc_truth_debt to catch source-of-truth documentation drift without weakening existing checks.
asi.baseline_reason: New metric retains quality_debt as a secondary monitor and adds stale review/policy signals; baseline shows only ARCHITECTURE.md Last reviewed is stale under this metric.
asi.adversarial_check: This is a benchmark expansion, not a shortcut: existing checks still run, quality_debt remains emitted, and the new debt points to a concrete maintenance contract in ARCHITECTURE.md.

description: Refresh architecture review date after full doc read

asi.hypothesis: After re-reading ARCHITECTURE.md end-to-end, updating its Last reviewed date removes real documentation-maintenance debt and satisfies the document's own maintenance contract.
asi.quality_guard: Read ARCHITECTURE.md fully before changing the date; run_experiment checks passed with the expanded static-lint gate.
asi.adversarial_check: This is not just date-gaming: the doc was read end-to-end in the session, and no content contradiction was found that required additional edits before bumping the review date.
asi.baseline_context: New doc_truth_debt baseline was 10 solely from stale_review_date=1; all other truthfulness and quality secondary metrics were already zero.

name: Autoresearch prompt, belief, and experiment guidance quality

asi.hypothesis: Since code/docs truth metrics are saturated, the next honest objective is improving persisted autoresearch guidance so future iterations carry explicit beliefs, assumptions, search goals, hypotheses, experiment queues, and realism guardrails.
asi.user_instruction: User asked to use recursive review to improve the prompt/goal/search goal/experiment and update beliefs, hypotheses, and assumptions while staying flexible and realistic.
asi.recursive_tool_note: RLM attempts failed in this environment (missing paths/certificate errors), so this baseline codifies the recursive-review target and leaves room for available subagents/manual review until RLM is usable.
asi.adversarial_check: This does not weaken existing checks: doc_truth_debt and quality_debt remain secondary monitors at zero, and correctness checks still run.
